content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 20, 1994

Clark E. Guinan
Assistant City Attorney
City of San Rafael
1400 Fifth Avenue
P.O. Box 151560
San Rafael, CA  94915-1560

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-047

Dear Mr. Guinan:

You have requested advice on behalf of San Rafael City
Councilmember David J. Zappetini regarding his duties and
obligations under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

The advice provided in this letter is based upon the facts
provided in your letters of February 10, 1994, May 26, 1994 and
August 29, 1994, as well as our telephone conversation on
September 19, 1994.

QUESTION

May Councilmember Zappetini participate in the city council
decision to amend the city's general plan to change land use
designations of the St. Vincent/Silveira property to permit
commercial, residential and high-density residential development?

CONCLUSION

Councilmember Zappetini may participate in the decision to
amend the general plan regarding land use designations for the
St. Vincent/Silveira development.

_________________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA   95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-047
Page 2

FACTS

Mr. Zappetini is an officer of Steve Zappetini & Son, Inc.
("Corporation") and has an ownership interest of fifty percent.
The corporation owns five parcels of real property within the
City of San Rafael, each with a value in excess of $100,000.
Mr. Zappetini individually owns two other parcels of real property
within the city, each with a fair market value in excess of
$100,000.

Mr. Zappetini's corporation is one of four steel fabricating
and ornamental iron businesses in the City of San Rafael, and is
one of twelve to fourteen such businesses in Marin County. The
corporation is awarded approximately three contracts out of every
one hundred jobs bid. The corporation does approximately ten to
fifteen percent of its work during a given year within the city
limits of San Rafael; this percentage has been consistent over the
last ten years.

The St. Vincent/Silveira project consists of several hundred
acres of mostly undeveloped property in the unincorporated area of
Marin County, which is in the City of San Rafael's sphere of
influence. The property is known locally as St. Vincent/Silveira,
named after the two owners of the property. The city's current
general plan includes land use designations and development
policies for the property.

A number of planning decisions and development approvals will
be made by the city council over the next year which will affect
the character of the permitted development of the property. These
decisions will impact the development of large multistory
commercial and residential buildings, which may require
reinforcing steel and may use ornamental iron.

The specific approvals and decisions before the city council
will include the following:

1) Approval of a general plan amendment, which if approved
in its present draft form, will involve changes to the land use
designations for the property, including the specific designation
of those areas that will be permitted to have nonresidential
(commercial) development, as well as those areas permitted to have
high density residential (apartment) development.

2) Approval of the owners' master plan and application for
pre-zoning of the property, which will indicate with more
specificity the character, distribution and density of residential
and nonresidential development to occur on the property,
consistent with the general plan amendment.

3) Approval of the owners' application for a tentative
subdivision map(s), which will establish the specific design and
improvement for the proposed parcels on which development will
occur.

File No. A-94-047
Page 3


4)  Certification of the environmental impact report or
approval or negative declarations or other environmental review
documents in connection with the foregoing approvals.

     The city council will discuss the general plan amendment at
its meeting on September 19, 1994.  Therefore, the advice given in
this letter will be limited only to Councilmember Zappetini's
participation in the upcoming decision regarding the general plan
amendment.

     Mr. Zappetini states that he does not have any business
connection with any of the proponents of the general plan
amendment for the project.  He has not received any income in the
last twelve months from any members of the family or from of the
St. Vincent's church.  A member of one of the Silveira families
contributed $400 to Mr. Zappetini's city council campaign.²
Mr. Zappetini donated under $100 in cash and approximately $200 in
his time to the church.

     The seven parcels of real property, five of which are owned
by the corporation and two of which are owned individually by
Mr. Zappetini, are all located four to five miles from the St.
Vincenzl/Silveira project.  Mr. Zappetini does not believe that
approval of the general plan amendment will have a reasonably
foreseeable financial effect of $10,000 or more on the fair market
value of any of the properties or will affect the rental value of
any of the property by $1,000 or more per twelve month period.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making, or using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official knows or
has reason to know the official has a financial interest.

     An official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate
family, or on:

          (a)  Any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth one thousand dollars
($1,000) or more.


_________________________________
2  The $400 contribution does not trigger a conflict of interest
since Mr. Zappetini is an elected official and is not a member of
an appointed board or commission.  (Section 84308.)

File No. A-94-047
Page 4


(b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

*****
Section 87103(a)-(e).

Mr. Zappetini has a fifty percent interest in Steve Zappetini
Son, Inc. and is an officer of the corporation. (Section
87103(a) and (d).)  Income of an individual also includes a pro
rata share of any income to any business entity or trust in which
the individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly or
beneficially, a 10-percent interest or greater. (Sections 82030
and 87103(c).)  Therefore, if it is reasonably foreseeable that
there will be a material financial effect on Zappetini & Son,
Inc., Mr. Zappetini may not participate in any decisions regarding
the general plan amendment.

Mr. Zappetini individually owns real property worth more than
$1,000 and the corporation owns real property worth more than
$1,000. (Section 87103(b).)  An interest in real property of an
individual also includes a pro rata share of interests in real
property of any business entity or trust in which the individual
or immediate family owns, directly, indirectly or beneficially, a
10-percent interest or greater. (Section 82033.)  Therefore, if
it is reasonably foreseeable that there will be a material
financial effect on any of the properties or on the corporation,
Mr. Zappetini may not participate in any decisions regarding the
general plan amendment.

File No. A-94-047
Page 5


Foreseeability

The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that they will occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision must be more than a mere
possibility; however, certainty is not required. (Downey Cares v.
Downey Community Development Com. (1987) 196 Cal. App. 3d 983,
989-991; Witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 Cal. App. 3d 817, 822; In re
Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.) The Act seeks to prevent more
than actual conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. (Witt v. Morrow,
supra at 823.)

The general plan amendment will determine the land use
designations for the St. Vincent/Silveira development. Such
designations could include commercial and high-density (apartment)
development, which will require reinforcing steel and may use
ornamental iron. Although a change in land use designations will
allow certain development that ultimately will require steel, the
principal question is whether it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision regarding this general plan amendment will have a
financial effect on Zappetini & Son, Inc.

A similar question was addressed in the Thorner Opinion,
where the Commission concluded that directors of a municipal water
district holding significant interests in business entities which
may be affected by the district's decisions on requests for
variances from a moratorium on new water connections may not
participate in the decisions on those requests when the decisions
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
the business entities in which the directors hold significant
interests. (Thorner, p. 4.)

The Commission then examined the individual interests of the
directors. The Commission found that there was not a reasonably
foreseeable financial effect on a director with no known
connection with the project, who may later bid on or supply
building materials to the project. (Thorner, p. 8.)

We would find Mr. Zappetini's situation to be analogous to
the director in Thorner. Since it is not reasonably foreseeable
that the decision to amend the general plan will have a financial
effect on Zappetini & Son, Inc., Councilmember Zappetini may
participate in this decision.

However, we are only advising on Councilmember Zappetini's
ability to participate in the general plan amendment decision
regarding the St. Vincent/Silveira project. Mr. Zappetini's
ability to participate in future decisions regarding this project
will have to be analyzed as each decision comes before the
council.

File No. A-94-047
Page 6


Materiality

     Mr. Zappetini individually owns two parcels of property
within the city and through the corporation he owns five parcels
of property within the city. All of the properties are located
four to five miles from the St. Vincent/Sildvira project. Mr.
Zappetini has stated that approval of the general plan amendment
will not have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect of $10,000
or more on the fair market value of any of the properties or will
not affect the rental value of any of the properties by $1,000 or
more in a twelve month period. (Regulation 18702.3(b).)

     Since the corporation owns five of the properties, you must
also ascertain if it is reasonably foreseeable that the general
plan amendment will have a material financial effect on the
corporation under Regulation 18702.2. Mr. Zappetini has indicated
that the general plan amendment will not materially affect the
corporation. Therefore, Mr. Zappetini may participate in the
decision regarding the general plan amendment.

     I trust this answers your question.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: [illegible]
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

QUESTION: May Councilmember Zappetini participate in the city council
decision to amend the city’s general plan to change land use
designations of the St. Vincent/Silveira property to permit
commercial, residential and high-density residential development?

CONCLUSION: Councilmember Zappetini may participate in the decision to
amend the general plan regarding land use designations for the
St. Vincent/Silveira development.
